text:
Market today: Rotation is the real story - money is moving away from semis and into defense/energy (headlines 1, 8). That's a headwind for your chip-heavy book. Sandisk and Micron sinking on rotation confirms semis are under pressure right now, even if supply shortages put a floor under them eventually.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the loudest alarm - it's a 3x leveraged ETF decaying against you and sitting only $17 above your stop; on margin, a bad day wipes that gap fast. SPAL (-13.72%) is brand-new, very volatile, and already near its tight $21.38 stop - tiny cushion, leveraged, dangerous. SMH (-2.5%) is the quieter cousin confirming the same sector rot.

Watch: Whether semis stabilize or the rotation accelerates - SMH holding above $600 is your canary. Also watch if CRWD (+7.4%) starts fading; it's your best winner and worth protecting.

Bottom line: Your margin exposure to leveraged semi ETFs is the single biggest risk today - manage those stops before anything else.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is your line - with -14.5% already in the hole and 3x decay grinding you, consider tightening that stop or trimming now before rotation bites harder.
SPAL: At -13.72% with a stop only $0.32 away at $21.38, this one could stop out today - decide now if you want to let the stop do its job or exit manually to control the exit price.
CRWD: Your only real winner at +7.4% - no action needed, but consider placing a mental stop near recent support to lock in some of that gain if the broad tech selloff deepens.
